findings.evidence: `WeilDivisor X` is just `X →₀ ℤ` in `WeilDivisor.lean`, and Mathlib has the general decomposition tool `Finsupp.sum_single` (`Mathlib/Algebra/BigOperators/Finsupp/Basic.lean:420`).
findings.fix: Add a general lemma such as `eq_sum_coeff_smul_ofPoint (D : WeilDivisor X) : D = ∑ x ∈ D.support, coeff D x • ofPoint x` and derive the `mapRange_natCast_*` lemmas from it.
findings.issue: `mapRange_natCast_eq_sum` and its degree/weighted-degree/pushforward consequences are stated only for `m : X →₀ ℕ`, but the natural Weil-divisor expansion is for every `D : WeilDivisor X`.

findings.evidence: The local helper `coeff_sum_zsmul_ofPoint_of_mem` already works for `{a : X → ℤ}`, and Mathlib provides the general `Finsupp.indicator_eq_sum_single` at `Mathlib/Algebra/BigOperators/Finsupp/Basic.lean:574`.
findings.fix: Prove the indicator sum/degree/weighted-degree/pushforward lemmas for arbitrary integer coefficients `a : X → ℤ`, then derive the natural-multiplicity and coefficient-one lemmas as special cases.
findings.issue: `indicator_nat_eq_sum`, `degree_indicator_nat`, `weightedDegree_indicator_nat`, and `pushforward_indicator_nat` are algebraic identities restricted to `m : X → ℕ`; effectivity only matters for the later positivity/zero criteria.

summary: Several finite-sum identities are specialized to effective/natural multiplicities even though the surrounding API and Mathlib support the natural integer-coefficient statements. Prove the general free-abelian-group forms first and keep the effective cases as derived specializations.
